skill.name: frontend-dev-guidelines
skill.description: Frontend development guidelines for React and TypeScript applications using Suspense, TanStack Query, TanStack Router, MUI v7, feature folders, and performance patterns.
skill.summary: Guide React teams toward consistent TypeScript, routing, data fetching, styling, and performance patterns.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium alerts, but manual review found Markdown documentation and TypeScript examples rather than executable skill code. The backtick, relative path, dynamic import, network, and weak-crypto detections are false positives in frontend guidance snippets. No prompt injection, exfiltration workflow, malicious command execution, or hidden runtime behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts come from Markdown code fences and inline TypeScript symbols, not Ruby backtick execution or shell invocation. The files are instructional content and do not define executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown checklist items and TypeScript fenced examples. No shell command API, script file, or runtime execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are Frontend Code Splitting Examples
low_findings.description: The dynamic import alerts show React.lazy and on-demand library loading examples. They are normal frontend performance patterns and do not load attacker-controlled module paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The module specifiers in reviewed examples are static strings for React components or known libraries. No user input controls import targets.

low_findings.title: Relative Import Paths Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts are relative imports and documentation links used to describe project organization. They do not read, write, delete, or enumerate filesystem paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sequences appear inside import examples such as ../api and a Markdown link. There is no filesystem API usage or untrusted path construction.

low_findings.title: API Client Mentions Are Application Guidance
low_findings.description: The network alerts describe using an existing frontend apiClient and route formats. They do not configure an external endpoint, transmit secrets, or add hidden network behavior to the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text is guidance for application code using a project axios client. No destination URL, credential collection, or exfiltration pattern was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: Manual review did not find cryptographic algorithms in the cited documentation context. The scanner appears to have matched unrelated text such as React.FC, headings, or ordinary frontend examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Keyword search and contextual review found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or crypto usage. The flagged lines are documentation text and React typing examples.

content.user_title: Build React Frontends with Consistent Patterns
content.value_statement: Frontend teams often lose time to inconsistent component, routing, and data-fetching choices. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear React and TypeScript patterns to apply.
actual_capabilities: Guides React component structure with React.FC, typed props, hooks, and default exports.
Recommends Suspense and useSuspenseQuery patterns for loading and data fetching.
Defines feature-folder organization for APIs, components, hooks, helpers, and types.
Shows MUI v7 styling patterns using sx, SxProps, Theme, and modern Grid syntax.
Documents TanStack Router route setup, lazy-loaded pages, and breadcrumb loaders.
Covers performance practices including useMemo, useCallback, React.memo, and debounced search.
limitations: It is a guidance skill, not a framework generator or runtime library.
Examples assume React, TypeScript, MUI, TanStack Query, and TanStack Router.
Project aliases such as @/ and ~features must already exist in the target codebase.
It does not verify backend APIs, authentication rules, or production deployment settings.

use_cases.title: Create a New Feature Module
use_cases.description: Use the feature folder, API service, typed hooks, and route guidance to scaffold consistent frontend feature code.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Review Criteria
use_cases.description: Use the patterns as review checkpoints for components, loading states, styling, imports, and TypeScript quality.

use_cases.title: Modernize Existing React Screens
use_cases.description: Convert older loading, routing, and data-fetching patterns to Suspense, lazy loading, and cache-aware TanStack Query usage.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Typed Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-dev-guidelines skill to create a typed React component for [purpose]. Follow the component structure and MUI styling guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Add Suspense Data Fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-dev-guidelines skill to add data fetching for [entity]. Use useSuspenseQuery, an API service layer, and a Suspense boundary.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Feature Route
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a complete feature slice.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-dev-guidelines skill to design a feature folder and TanStack Router route for [feature]. Include components, hooks, types, and API files.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor for Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-dev-guidelines skill to review this React screen for lazy loading, memoization, loading states, import aliases, and TypeScript issues.

output_examples.input: Create a user profile page with data fetching.
output_examples.output: A plan for a lazy-loaded route, a typed API service, a Suspense-wrapped profile component, and MUI sx-based layout.

output_examples.input: Review this table component for frontend guideline issues.
output: Recommends memoized filtering and stable callbacks.

output_examples.input: Organize a posts feature in a React app.
output_examples.output: A feature layout with api, components, hooks, helpers, types, public exports, and a TanStack Router route.

best_practices: Use the skill when changing React, TypeScript, routing, styling, or data-fetching code.
Provide the existing project aliases and component conventions before asking for generated code.
Ask for a guideline review before merging large frontend changes.
anti_patterns: Do not apply the examples to non-React or non-TypeScript projects without adaptation.
Do not copy project-specific aliases unless the target app defines them.
Do not treat documentation examples as security-reviewed production code.

faq.question: What frontend stack does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets React with TypeScript, MUI v7, TanStack Query, TanStack Router, and Suspense-based loading.

faq.question: Can it generate complete frontend features?
faq.answer: Yes, it can guide feature structure and code patterns when the target project uses the expected stack.

faq.question: Does it replace project-specific architecture decisions?
faq.answer: No. It provides defaults that should be reconciled with local architecture and team conventions.

faq.question: Why does it mention API clients?
faq.answer: The data-fetching guidance assumes an existing project apiClient for consistent requests and error handling.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Manual review found documentation examples, not executable malicious behavior. The audit rates it low risk.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
